# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Jenkins Pipeline that runs directly on the master node for faster execution. The pipeline performs builds, runs tests, and deploys to production. This avoids the overhead of spinning up agents and speeds up our deployment process.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent { label 'master' }
    
    environment {
        APP_NAME = 'myapp'
        BUILD_DIR = 'build'
        DEPLOY_DIR = '/var/www/production'
        JAVA_HOME = '/usr/lib/jvm/java-11-openjdk'
        MAVEN_HOME = '/usr/share/maven'
    }
    
    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                checkout scm
                echo "Checked out code on master node"
            }
        }
        
        stage('Build') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    echo "Building application..."
                    mkdir -p ${BUILD_DIR}
                    ${MAVEN_HOME}/bin/mvn clean package -DskipTests
                    cp target/*.jar ${BUILD_DIR}/
                '''
            }
        }
        
        stage('Unit Tests') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    echo "Running unit tests..."
                    ${MAVEN_HOME}/bin/mvn test
                '''
                junit '**/target/surefire-reports/*.xml'
            }
        }
        
        stage('Integration Tests') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    echo "Running integration tests..."
                    ${MAVEN_HOME}/bin/mvn verify -Pintegration-tests
                '''
            }
        }
        
        stage('Code Quality') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    echo "Running code quality checks..."
                    ${MAVEN_HOME}/bin/mvn sonar:sonar
                '''
            }
        }
        
        stage('Deploy to Production') {
            steps {
                input message: 'Deploy to production?', ok: 'Deploy'
                sh '''
                    echo "Deploying to production..."
                    sudo systemctl stop ${APP_NAME} || true
                    sudo mkdir -p ${DEPLOY_DIR}
                    sudo cp ${BUILD_DIR}/*.jar ${DEPLOY_DIR}/
                    sudo chown -R appuser:appgroup ${DEPLOY_DIR}
                    sudo systemctl start ${APP_NAME}
                '''
            }
        }
        
        stage('Health Check') {
            steps {
                sh '''
                    echo "Performing health check..."
                    sleep 10
                    curl -f http://localhost:8080/health || exit 1
                    echo "Deployment successful!"
                '''
            }
        }
    }
    
    post {
        success {
            echo 'Pipeline completed successfully!'
            emailext(
                to: 'team@example.com',
                subject: "SUCCESS: ${env.JOB_NAME} - ${env.BUILD_NUMBER}",
                body: "Build succeeded. Check console output at ${env.BUILD_URL}"
            )
        }
        failure {
            echo 'Pipeline failed!'
            emailext(
                to: 'team@example.com',
                subject: "FAILURE: ${env.JOB_NAME} - ${env.BUILD_NUMBER}",
                body: "Build failed. Check console output at ${env.BUILD_URL}"
            )
        }
        always {
            cleanWs()
        }
    }
}
